pattern: Shrinking each query range inward to its nearest enclosing candles and answering with a prefix count of plates turns every range query into O(1) work after one linear pass.
hints: Only the candles that bound a query matter: any plate outside the span between the leftmost and rightmost candle in range can't count.
Precompute, for every index, the closest candle at or after it and the closest candle at or before it, plus a running total of plates seen so far.

For a query, shrink to the nearest candle >= left and the nearest candle <= right; if that left candle is before that right candle, the answer is the prefix plate count between them, otherwise it's 0.

edge_inputs.description: only one candle in range, no room for plates between two candles
minimal string length with a plate flanked by candles at both ends
two adjacent candles with zero plates between them
plates scattered between two candles with plates outside the span too
no candles anywhere in the string
combination of queries including one whose shrunk span is empty
query span collapses onto a single candle position

stress.description: random plate/candle string with many range queries
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, min(size, 100000))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice('*|') for _ in range(n))
    q = max(1, min(200, n))
    queries = []
    for _ in range(q):
        l = rng.randint(0, n - 1)
        r = rng.randint(l, n - 1)
        queries.append([l, r])
    return [s, queries]
stress.note: query count capped so the brute-force oracle (O(n) per query) stays well under the time budget
